app.name: UmlautAdaptarrEX
app.tagline: Umlaut + German title proxy for Sonarr / Radarr / Lidarr / Readarr

setup.title: First-run setup
setup.intro: Create the admin user, connect Prowlarr, and configure the HTTP-Proxy credentials.
setup.step1Hint: These credentials sign you in to the admin UI.
setup.step2Hint: Enter the URL and API key of your Prowlarr instance, we'll discover Sonarr / Radarr / Lidarr / Readarr automatically.
setup.step3Hint: Provide the API key for each app you want to manage. Test the connection before continuing.
setup.step4Hint: Set the Basic-Auth credentials Prowlarr (and other indexer clients) will use to connect to the local HTTP-Proxy on this port.
setup.step5Hint: Optional, push the proxy entry to Prowlarr automatically. You can do this later from Settings.
setup.modeHint: Choose how UmlautAdaptarr should work. You can change this later in Settings.
setup.modeProxyDescription: Only the HTTP proxy on port {proxyPort} is active. The legacy indexer API on port {legacyApiPort} returns a hint message instead.
setup.modeProxyDetails: Recommended for new setups. Prowlarr connects through the HTTP proxy, which rewrites indexer responses transparently. Sonarr/Radarr need no extra API keys because rewriting happens at search time.
setup.modeLegacyDescription: Only the legacy indexer API on port {legacyApiPort} is active. Port {proxyPort} returns a hint message to every connection.
setup.modeLegacyDetails: Wire-compatible with the .NET predecessor. Sonarr/Radarr hit {legacyApiPort}/'<apiKey>/<host>'/api directly. Choose this mode if you're migrating an existing 1.x install and want URLs to stay stable.
setup.modeBothDescription: Both the proxy ({proxyPort}) and the legacy API ({legacyApiPort}) are active.
setup.modeBothDetails: Run both paths in parallel. Only useful during a staged migration from legacy to proxy mode. In steady-state operation it doubles the attack surface for no benefit.
setup.modeBothWarning: Not recommended — usually only one of the two paths is needed at a time.
setup.pluginsHint: Choose the language plugins used to generate search variations and rewrite indexer responses. You can change this later in Settings.
setup.testProwlarrOk: Connection OK, {count, plural, =0 {no apps detected} one {# app detected} other {# apps detected}}.
setup.installHostRequired: Please enter a host so Prowlarr can reach the proxy.
setup.installOverwriteWarning: An existing entry named "UmlautAdaptarrEX" will be overwritten.
setup.installHostHint: How Prowlarr reaches the proxy. Adjust if Prowlarr runs in a different network or container.
setup.tmdbApiKey: TMDB API key (optional, used for multi-language titles in Radarr/Sonarr)
setup.tmdbApiKeyHint: Required as soon as you enable a non-German language plugin (Swedish, French …). We need the v3 API key (32-char hex).

tmdbTestErr.v4_token: Detected v4 Read Access Token (JWT). Please use the v3 API key (32-char hex).
tmdbTestErr.invalid_format: The key looks malformed, expected the v3 API key (32-char hex).
tmdbTestErr.unauthorized: TMDB rejected the key (401). Is this the right v3 API key?

setup.tvdbApiKey: TVDB v4 API key (optional, for TVDB-based titles in Sonarr/Radarr)
setup.tvdbApiKeyHint: API key from your TVDB account. Optionally pair with a Subscriber-PIN if your account requires one.
setup.tvdbPinPlaceholder: Only if your TVDB account uses a PIN
setup.tvdbPinHint: Only required for subscriber-only endpoints. Leave empty if the standard API key is enough.

tvdbTestErr.unauthorized: TVDB rejected the key (401). Are the API key and (if needed) PIN correct?

setup.pluginsTmdbBanner: Non-German language plugins need a TMDB API key. Add it in step 1, without it those checkboxes stay locked.
setup.pluginsTmdbRequired: Add a TMDB API key in step 1 first, then you can enable this plugin.
setup.proxyPasswordHint: Auto-generated on first load. Use the refresh icon to roll a new one. Minimum 8 characters.
setup.syncStepHint: {count, plural, one {One instance was connected} other {# instances were connected}}. Do you want to run a first sync now?
setup.syncStepBody: The sync pulls the search entries of the connected instances and indexes them for umlaut handling. You can skip this and start a sync later from the dashboard at any time.

login.title: Sign in
login.subtitle: Use your admin credentials to continue.
login.rateLimited: Too many attempts. Please wait a few minutes.
login.sessionExpired: Session expired, please sign in again.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Overview of sync activity and instance health.
dashboard.syncDoneWithErrors: Sync finished with errors: {items} items, {errors} instance(s) failed.
dashboard.syncConfirmBody: This will refresh search items from {count, plural, one {# enabled instance} other {# enabled instances}}.
dashboard.syncProgressHint: You can close this dialog, the sync continues in the background.
dashboard.instancesSubtitle: Status at a glance, click a card to manage.
dashboard.noInstancesHint: Connect Sonarr, Radarr, Lidarr or Readarr to get started.
dashboard.noRunsHint: Trigger your first sync to populate this list.

actions.needsProwlarr: Connect Prowlarr in Settings to use this action.

charts.requestsHint: Hourly split by cache hits and misses.

instances.title: Arr instances
instances.subtitle: Manage Sonarr / Radarr / Lidarr / Readarr connections.
instances.listSubtitle: Toggle the switch to enable or pause syncing.
instances.addSubtitle: Connect Sonarr, Radarr, Lidarr or Readarr.
instances.editSubtitle: Update connection details for this instance.
instances.name: Name
instances.hostHint: Full URL including protocol, e.g. http://sonarr:8989
instances.apiKeyMaskedHint: The stored value is only the Prowlarr mask (********). Enter the real API key from Sonarr/Radarr/Lidarr/Readarr.
instances.enabledHint: Disabled instances are skipped during sync.
instances.providerOrderHint: Walked top to bottom on every sync. pcjones returns German titles only; TVDB and TMDB serve any language. Drag with the handle or use arrow keys to reorder.
instances.enableYearMatchingHint: When enabled, the matcher rejects releases whose 4-digit year token in the filename lies outside the tolerance window around the release/first-air year from Sonarr/Radarr. Prevents e.g. a sports-event recording from being rewritten as a same-named movie released in a different year. Disable this if your library years are unreliable and legitimate rewrites are being suppressed.
instances.yearMatchingToleranceHint: 0 = strict equality, 1 = +/-1 year tolerance (default), up to 5. Absorbs production-vs-release-year skew.
instances.noInstances: Add your first Sonarr/Radarr/Lidarr/Readarr to get started.
instances.deleteConfirm: This permanently removes "{name}" and its cached search items. This cannot be undone.

prowlarr.dialogDescription: Connect to a running Prowlarr and import its configured apps. Re-running updates host and API key for matching instances.
prowlarr.apiKeyPlaceholder: Found in Prowlarr → Settings → General → Security

prowlarr.apiKeyTooShort: API key must be at least 8 characters.
prowlarr.apiKeyStoredHint: Stored, leave empty to reuse the saved key.
prowlarr.storedCredsHint: Prowlarr connection is already saved. You can keep the host and leave the API key field empty to reuse the stored key.
prowlarr.rowOpenApiKeyPage: Open API key page for {name} (opens in a new tab)
prowlarr.noValidSelections: No valid selections, please enter API keys.
prowlarr.partialImport: {count, plural, one {# selection without API key, skipped} other {# selections without API key, skipped}}
prowlarr.previewSubtitle: Pick which apps to import. Existing instances with the same type + name will be updated.
prowlarr.previewSelected: {count, plural, =0 {None selected} one {# selected} other {# selected}}
prowlarr.submit: Import {count, plural, =0 {nothing} one {# app} other {# apps}}
prowlarr.sessionExpired: Your session expired, please reload and sign in again.
prowlarr.emptyNoConfigBody: Set up your Prowlarr connection in the Settings page first, then come back here to import.
prowlarr.emptyAuthBody: Prowlarr returned 401, re-enter the API key on the Settings page.
prowlarr.emptyFetchBody: Check the host and connectivity on the Settings page.
prowlarr.overwriteBody: {count, plural, one {# existing instance} other {# existing instances}} will be overwritten with host and API key from Prowlarr. This cannot be undone.

skippedReasons.missing_api_key: Prowlarr did not return an API key, add manually
skippedReasons.masked_api_key: Prowlarr returns masked API keys only (********). Add the instance manually or fill in the key later.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Configure providers, the indexer cache and the application API key.

section.providersHint: External services used to look up German titles.
section.advancedHint: Use the defaults unless you know what you're doing.

plugins.title: Built-in plugins
plugins.hint: Each plugin contributes a set of language-specific transformations (e.g. ä → ae) used to generate search variations and rewrite indexer responses.
plugins.resyncBanner: Plugin changes only take effect after a sync, your library variations were generated with the previous configuration.
plugins.resyncConflict: A sync is already running, or no instances are active.
plugins.tmdbKeyRequired: Non-German language plugins (Swedish, French, …) require a TMDB API key, pcjones only returns German titles. Add a v3 API key (32-char hex) in the Providers tab.
plugins.tmdbKeyRequiredToast: This plugin needs a TMDB API key. Add it in the Providers tab first.

settings.appApiKeyHint: Used by Prowlarr / *arrs to authenticate against UmlautAdaptarrEX.
settings.regenerateConfirm: The current key will stop working immediately. Update Prowlarr and your *arrs with the new key.
settings.legacyApiPortHint: Current port of the Fastify API (API, legacy indexer routes, log stream). Set via the UMLAUTADAPTARREX_LEGACYAPI_PORT environment variable; takes effect after a server restart.
settings.webUiPortHint: Current port of the web interface. Set via the UMLAUTADAPTARREX_WEBUI_PORT environment variable; takes effect after a server restart.
settings.proxyPortHint: Takes effect after a server restart.
settings.proxyPortEnvManagedHint: Set via the UMLAUTADAPTARREX_PROXY_PORT environment variable; change it there, not here.

proxyAuth.title: HTTP-Proxy credentials
proxyAuth.hint: Basic-Auth credentials Prowlarr (and other indexers) must use when connecting to the HTTP-Proxy on this port. Configure the same username and password in Prowlarr → Indexers → Proxy.
proxyAuth.passwordHint: Leave the field unchanged to keep the current password. Minimum 8 characters when setting a custom one.
proxyAuth.regenerateConfirm: The current proxy password stops working immediately. Update Prowlarr (and any other indexer client) with the new password afterwards.

settings.tmdbApiKey: TMDB API key (for multi-language titles in Radarr/Sonarr)
settings.tmdbApiKeyHint: We need the classic v3 API key (32-char hex string, listed as 'API Key (v3 auth)' in TMDB). v4 Read Access Tokens (JWT, starting with 'eyJ…') are not supported, please copy the v3 key from your TMDB account settings.

tmdbTestErr.missing: No API key stored or entered.
tmdbTestErr.v4_token: Detected v4 Read Access Token (JWT). Please use the v3 API key (32-char hex).
tmdbTestErr.invalid_format: The key looks malformed, expected the v3 API key (32-char hex).
tmdbTestErr.unauthorized: TMDB rejected the key (401). Is this the right v3 API key?

settings.tvdbApiKey: TVDB v4 API key (for TVDB-based titles in Sonarr/Radarr)
settings.tvdbApiKeyHint: API key from your TVDB account. Optionally pair with a Subscriber-PIN if your account requires one.
settings.tvdbPinPlaceholder: Only if your TVDB account uses a PIN
settings.tvdbPinHint: Only required for subscriber-only endpoints. Leave empty if your standard API key is enough.

tvdbTestErr.missing: No API key stored or entered.
tvdbTestErr.unauthorized: TVDB rejected the key (401). Are the API key and PIN correct?

settings.logRetentionDaysHint: Logs older than this are deleted by a cleanup job that runs every 6 hours. 1–30 days.
settings.indexerRateLimitMsHint: Minimum spacing between requests to the same indexer host. Lower = faster TV variation searches but higher risk of getting throttled (HTTP 429) or banned by free-tier indexers. Default 500 ms. The limiter automatically backs off when an indexer responds with Retry-After.
settings.indexerTimeoutSecondsHint: Maximum wait per indexer request, applied to TCP connect, response headers, and body alike. Raise this if you see ConnectTimeoutError / UND_ERR_CONNECT_TIMEOUT for slow indexers; lower it if a stalled indexer is blocking Sonarr/Radarr searches. Default 60 seconds, range 5–600.
settings.blockPrivateInstanceHostsHint: Only enable this when UmlautAdaptarrEX is publicly reachable. It refuses connections to private/loopback addresses (localhost, 192.168.*, 10.*, *.local, host.docker.internal …) as an SSRF safeguard. Self-hosted setups with Sonarr/Radarr on the same LAN or Docker network should leave this off. The env vars UA_BLOCK_PRIVATE_INSTANCE_HOSTS / UA_ALLOW_PRIVATE_INSTANCE_HOSTS override this toggle at boot.

titleCache.title: Title cache
titleCache.hint: Resolved German titles are stored in the database so each sync only asks the provider for unknown titles. Clear the cache when provider data has changed or you want to force a fresh sync.
titleCache.stats: {total, plural, =0 {Cache is empty} one {# entry} other {# entries}}, {positive} resolved, {negative} negative.
titleCache.clearConfirm: All stored provider responses are removed. The next sync will refetch every title from the provider.
titleCache.cleared: Title cache cleared ({count, plural, =0 {no entries} one {# entry} other {# entries}}).
titleCache.recheckHint: Re-asks the currently configured providers (pcjones / TVDB / TMDB) for every cache entry that is marked as 'not found' or has language gaps. Useful after adding an API key or reordering providers.
titleCache.recheckNoneMissing: No missing titles in the cache.
titleCache.recheckDone: {checked, plural, one {# entry} other {# entries}} re-checked, {recovered} recovered, {stillMissing} still missing.

operationMode.restartHint: Note: the legacy API on port {legacyApiPort} switches immediately. The listener on port {proxyPort} only re-binds on the next start.
operationMode.restartPending: Mode saved. Server restart required for the port {proxyPort} listener to pick up the new mode.

restart.title: Restart server
restart.hint: Restarts the Fastify gateway and the web UI. In-flight connections drop briefly; the UI reloads automatically once the server is back.
restart.confirmHint: The server will shut down and start back up automatically. In-flight requests are aborted.
restart.waitingHint: Waiting for the server… the UI will reload automatically once it answers.
restart.timeout: Server did not respond within 30 seconds. Please check logs and restart manually if needed.
restart.unsupported: Restart from the UI is not available in this environment (dev mode or no supervisor). Please restart the container/service manually.
restart.unsupportedTitle: Restart not available — please restart manually.

prowlarr.title: Prowlarr connection
prowlarr.hint: Save the Prowlarr URL and API key once. The Import dialog and re-syncs reuse this connection, no need to re-enter it.
prowlarr.apiKeyPlaceholder: Found in Prowlarr → Settings → General → Security
prowlarr.apiKeyPlaceholderStored: Enter the API key again to overwrite the stored one
prowlarr.apiKeyHint: Found under Settings → General → Security in Prowlarr.
prowlarr.apiKeyHintStored: A key is stored. Re-enter it here only when changing it, leave empty to keep the existing one.
prowlarr.testOk: Connection OK, {count, plural, =0 {no apps detected} one {# app detected} other {# apps detected}}.
prowlarr.saved: Saved, {count, plural, =0 {no apps} one {# app} other {# apps}} reachable in Prowlarr.
prowlarr.disconnectConfirm: Stored host and API key will be deleted. Imports will need fresh credentials afterwards.
prowlarr.installDescription: Creates an indexer proxy "UmlautAdaptarrEX" and a tag "umlautadaptarrex" in your Prowlarr. Apply the tag to indexers in Prowlarr afterwards to route them through the proxy.
prowlarr.installOverwriteWarning: An existing entry named "UmlautAdaptarrEX" will be overwritten.
prowlarr.installHostHint: How Prowlarr reaches our proxy. Adjust if Prowlarr runs in a different network or container.
prowlarr.installPasswordHint: Pushed from your stored server settings.
prowlarr.installNoPasswordError: No proxy password is set. Configure it in the application settings first.
prowlarr.patchDescription: Select the indexers that should be patched for UmlautAdaptarrEX.
prowlarr.patchWhyBody: By default Prowlarr talks to indexers over https://. That sends the request through the proxy as an encrypted tunnel, so the title cannot be rewritten. Patching switches the indexer address in Prowlarr to http:// so the request flows through the local proxy as plain text and the umlaut variations can be applied.
prowlarr.patchSafeBody: The http:// only affects the local hop from Prowlarr to the UmlautAdaptarrEX proxy (usually localhost or your own network). The connection to the indexer on the internet always stays https://, hard-wired in the code. So no unencrypted traffic leaves your system.
prowlarr.patchNotPatchable: No editable base URL — cannot be patched.

changelog.dialogIntro: These changes shipped with the latest update.
changelog.sectionHint: Every published update at a glance.

germanUmlauts.name: German Umlauts
germanUmlauts.description: Generates Latin equivalents (ä → ae, ö → oe, ü → ue, ß → ss) and dot-stripped variants (ä → a, …) plus German+English article stripping (Der/Die/Das/The/An/A).

swedishUmlauts.name: Swedish Umlauts
swedishUmlauts.description: Generates Swedish romanizations: Å → A or AA, Ä → A or AE, Ö → O or OE (case-preserving).

frenchAccents.name: French Accents
frenchAccents.description: Strips French accents (é → e, à → a, ç → c, …) and expands ligatures (æ → ae, œ → oe). Also strips French articles (Le/La/Les/Un/Une/Des/Du/De).
